agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), tiered account verification, and fraud prevention controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction finality are legally mandated.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process required before a user can send money or withdraw funds. Determines account verification tier."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has fully completed KYC. Eligible for sending, receiving, and withdrawing funds up to a $5,000 daily transfer limit."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account with incomplete KYC that has passed some verification steps. Can send transfers up to a $500 daily limit but cannot withdraw."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account that has not begun or completed any KYC. Can only receive funds — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The point at which a transaction becomes final and irreversible, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation)."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on regulatory sanctions lists (e.g., OFAC). All transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. The account becomes inaccessible until a support agent manually unlocks it."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum aggregate outbound transfer amount permitted within a rolling or calendar day, determined by verification tier."},{"term":"Transaction Notification","definition":"An alert sent to the user for any transaction exceeding $1,000 in value."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A user must complete full KYC identity verification before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement. Allowing unverified users to send money violates AML/KYC law and exposes the platform to legal liability. This is not a policy preference — it is a compliance gate.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to initiate an outbound transfer from an unverified account MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw funds.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State integrity constraint — the account verification tier directly governs permissible operations. Violating this corrupts the trust model and regulatory posture.","testable_assertion":"An inbound transfer to an unverified account MUST succeed. An outbound transfer or withdrawal from an unverified account MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Verified accounts have a daily transfer limit of $5,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy threshold. A VP or compliance officer could adjust this limit based on risk appetite or market conditions without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a verified account within one day MUST NOT exceed $5,000. Any transfer that would exceed this limit MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Partially-verified accounts have a daily transfer limit of $500.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy threshold tied to risk tier. Adjustable by business decision without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a partially-verified account within one day MUST NOT exceed $500. Any transfer that would exceed this limit MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Transaction finality is a core financial integrity constraint. If settled transactions could be reversed, ledger consistency, reconciliation, and trust are destroyed. This is a hard constraint of the clearing model.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction whose settlement timestamp is in the past (>=T+1) MUST be rejected. Pre-settlement transactions MAY be reversible."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal/regulatory mandate. Failure to block sanctioned transfers exposes the company to criminal liability, fines, and license revocation. This is not discretionary.","testable_assertion":"Any outbound transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned entity list MUST be blocked before funds move. The transaction MUST NOT reach pending or settled state."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Five consecutive failed login attempts lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity constraint. The lock state must be enforced consistently — if the system allows a 6th attempt or auto-unlocks, the fraud prevention mechanism is compromised. The manual-unlock-only requirement prevents automated bypass attacks.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account MUST be locked. A 6th login attempt with correct credentials MUST still be rejected. Only a support agent unlock action MUST restore access."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business/UX policy. The threshold and notification channel could be changed by product decision. Failing to notify does not corrupt data or violate law (unless jurisdiction-specific rules apply).","testable_assertion":"Any transaction with amount > $1,000 MUST trigger a notification to the account holder. Transactions of exactly $1,000 or less MUST NOT trigger this notification."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"They attempt to send money","then":"The transfer is rejected with a clear message requiring KYC completion"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"Another user sends them money","then":"The transfer succeeds and funds are credited to the unverified account balance"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"They attempt to withdraw funds","then":"The withdrawal is rejected with a message requiring KYC completion"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified account that has already sent $4,500 today","when":"The user attempts to send $600","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A verified account that has already sent $4,500 today","when":"The user attempts to send $500","then":"The transfer succeeds, bringing the daily total to $5,000"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A partially-verified account that has sent $0 today","when":"The user attempts to send $501","then":"The transfer is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction was initiated yesterday and has settled (T+1)","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with a message that settled transactions are final"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A transaction was initiated today and has not yet settled","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal may be permitted (pre-settlement window)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user initiates a transfer to a recipient","when":"The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked immediately, funds are not moved, and the event is logged for compliance"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A user has entered 4 consecutive incorrect passwords","when":"They enter a 5th incorrect password","then":"The account is locked and the user is informed that support must unlock it"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A locked account","when":"The user attempts to log in with the correct password","then":"Login is rejected and the account remains locked until a support agent unlocks it"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A locked account","when":"A support agent performs an unlock action","then":"The account is unlocked and the user can log in again with valid credentials"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A user with a verified account","when":"They complete a transaction of $1,001","then":"A notification is sent to the account holder"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A user with a verified account","when":"They complete a transaction of exactly $1,000","then":"No notification is triggered under the >$1,000 rule"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A user has entered 4 consecutive incorrect passwords then 1 correct password","when":"They subsequently enter 4 more incorrect passwords","then":"The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Partially-verified account attempts to withdraw","expected_behavior":"Spec says unverified cannot withdraw, but is silent on partially-verified withdrawals. Likely should also be blocked until full KYC, but this is ambiguous."},{"scenario":"Transfer amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"No notification is sent. The rule states 'over $1,000', meaning strictly greater than. $1,000.00 does not trigger notification; $1,000.01 does."},{"scenario":"Transfer to a sanctioned entity from an unverified account that is only receiving","expected_behavior":"If a sanctioned entity sends money TO a user, the spec only mentions blocking transfers TO sanctioned entities. Inbound from sanctioned entities is unaddressed but almost certainly must also be blocked per compliance."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary at midnight — timezone question","expected_behavior":"The spec says 'daily' but does not define the day boundary. Is it UTC? User's local timezone? Account timezone? This must be clarified."},{"scenario":"Recipient becomes sanctioned between transaction initiation and settlement","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether the sanctions check occurs only at initiation or also at settlement. Best practice is to check at both points."},{"scenario":"Account locked, then user completes KYC or changes password","expected_behavior":"Lock persists regardless — only a support agent can unlock. Self-service password reset does not bypass the lock."},{"scenario":"Simultaneous transfers that individually are within daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"Race condition: the system must enforce the aggregate daily limit atomically. Two $3,000 transfers submitted concurrently from a verified account must not both succeed."},{"scenario":"Notification delivery failure for >$1,000 transaction","expected_behavior":"Spec says notification must be sent but does not define retry policy or whether transaction should proceed if notification fails. Transaction should likely proceed; notification is best-effort."},{"scenario":"Partially-verified user's failed count carries across verification status change","expected_behavior":"If a user has 4 failed attempts while unverified, then completes partial KYC, the failed login counter should not reset — verification status change is not a login event."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Direct revenue impact: transfer limits cap transaction volume and fee income per user. Sanctions blocking prevents catastrophic regulatory fines (potentially millions). Settlement finality protects against double-spend and fraud losses.","operational":"KYC verification creates onboarding friction — conversion rate depends on smooth verification flow. Account locking generates support ticket volume; every locked account requires manual agent intervention. Sanctions list must be kept current, requiring integration with OFAC/EU lists and regular updates.","user":"Unverified users have a degraded experience (receive-only). Lock-on-5-failures balances security with usability — too aggressive for some users but standard in fintech. Notifications for >$1,000 provide transparency and fraud awareness.","risk":"HIGH — sanctions compliance failure is existential risk (license revocation, criminal liability). KYC bypass is a money-laundering vector. Settlement reversal bugs create financial exposure. Account lock bypass enables credential-stuffing attacks.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users","impact":"Must complete KYC to access full functionality. Subject to daily limits and account locking. Receive transaction notifications."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal","impact":"Directly affected by sanctions screening accuracy, KYC enforcement, and transaction finality. Regulatory reporting depends on correct rule enforcement."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Account unlocking is a manual process — volume of locked accounts directly impacts support capacity and cost."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality (T+1) affects reconciliation, float management, and liquidity planning."},{"group":"Product / Growth","impact":"KYC friction and transfer limits affect conversion and retention. Partially-verified tier is a growth lever to reduce drop-off."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must implement atomic daily limit enforcement, real-time sanctions screening, and idempotent settlement logic."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is reasonably clear on most rules but has several ambiguities: (1) partially-verified withdrawal rights are not explicitly stated, (2) 'daily' limit timezone/boundary is undefined, (3) inbound transfers from sanctioned entities are not addressed, (4) pre-settlement reversal is implied but not explicitly permitted, (5) notification delivery guarantees are unspecified. No contradictions exist. Some rules touch regulatory/legal territory (sanctions, KYC) which would benefit from legal review to confirm jurisdictional requirements.","high_confidence_areas":["KYC gate on sending money","Unverified accounts can receive but not send","Settlement finality at T+1","Account lock after 5 consecutive failures requiring support unlock","Sanctions blocking on outbound transfers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw","Daily limit day boundary definition (timezone)","Inbound transfer handling for sanctioned senders","Pre-settlement reversal mechanics","Notification delivery guarantees and failure handling"],"assumptions":["'Daily' means a calendar day in some consistent timezone (likely UTC or account timezone)","Partially-verified accounts cannot withdraw (inferred from the KYC requirement but not explicitly stated)","The sanctions list is an external feed (e.g., OFAC SDN) that must be checked in real-time","Notification channel is unspecified — assumed to be push notification, email, or SMS per user preference","'Transaction over $1,000' means strictly greater than $1,000.00","Failed login counter resets on successful login but not on any other account event"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can partially-verified accounts withdraw funds? The spec states unverified accounts cannot withdraw and defines a $500 daily send limit for partially-verified, but never explicitly addresses partially-verified withdrawal rights.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance / Legal","Product / Growth"],"options":["A: Partially-verified can withdraw up to their $500 daily limit","B: Partially-verified cannot withdraw — full KYC required for any withdrawal","C: Partially-verified can withdraw with a separate, lower limit"],"recommendation":"Option B — require full KYC for withdrawals. Withdrawals are a higher-risk money movement and should require the highest verification tier. This is standard in fintech."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"What defines a 'day' for daily transfer limits? No timezone or day boundary is specified.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Engineering","Compliance / Legal"],"options":["A: UTC calendar day","B: User's local timezone","C: Rolling 24-hour window from first transfer"],"recommendation":"Option A — UTC calendar day. Simplest to implement consistently, avoids timezone manipulation, and is standard in financial systems."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Inbound transfers from sanctioned entities are not addressed. The spec only covers outbound transfers TO sanctioned entities. Regulatory compliance almost certainly requires blocking inbound as well.","affected_stakeholders":["Compliance / Legal","End Users","Finance / Treasury"],"options":["A: Block inbound transfers from sanctioned entities","B: Allow inbound but flag for review","C: Only block outbound per current spec"],"recommendation":"Option A — block inbound transfers from sanctioned entities. Receiving funds from sanctioned parties is equally illegal. This must be confirmed with legal counsel."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), tiered account verification, and fraud prevention controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction finality are legally mandated.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"I
